mission.title: Install and Configure Artifact Hub on Kubernetes
mission.description: Artifact Hub is a web-based application that enables finding, installing, and publishing Cloud Native packages. Installing it allows you to easily discover and manage various artifacts for your CNCF projects in a centralized manner.

steps.title: Add the Bitnami Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Bitnami repository which contains the PostgreSQL dependency needed for Artifact Hub.
```bash
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Artifact Hub using Helm
steps.description:
Install Artifact Hub with the specified version in a new namespace called 'artifact-hub'.
```bash
helm install artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --version 1.22.1-3 --namespace artifact-hub --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Artifact Hub pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace artifact-hub
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Edit the values.yaml file to set resource limits for the Artifact Hub deployment. You can use the following command to set limits directly:
```bash
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi
```

steps.title: Access the Artifact Hub
steps.description:
Port-forward to access the Artifact Hub UI locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub 8080:80
```

steps.title: Open Artifact Hub in your browser
steps.description: Open your web browser and navigate to http://localhost:8080 to access the Artifact Hub interface.

resolution.summary: A successful installation will show the Artifact Hub pods in a Running state when you execute the `kubectl get pods` command. You should also be able to access the Artifact Hub UI at http://localhost:8080.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add bitnami https://charts.bitnami.com/bitnami
helm repo update
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub --set resources.limits.cpu=500m --set resources.limits.memory=512Mi

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Artifact Hub release from the cluster using Helm. This will remove all associated resources except for CRDs and persistent volumes.
```bash
helm uninstall artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and persistent resources
uninstall.description:
Remove any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) and persistent volume claims (PVCs) associated with Artifact Hub to ensure a clean uninstallation.
```bash
kubectl delete crd artifacthubrepositories.artifacthub.io
kubectl delete crd artifacthubcharts.artifacthub.io
kubectl delete pvc --all --namespace artifact-hub
```

uninstall.title: Remove namespace and verify
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'artifact-hub' namespace and verify that it has been removed from the cluster.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace artifact-hub
kubectl get namespaces
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of your Artifact Hub installation. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
helm get values artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub > artifact-hub-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update repo and run upgrade command
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository and upgrade the Artifact Hub installation to the latest version. Replace '1.22.2-0' with the desired version.
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade artifact-hub artifact-hub/artifact-hub --version 1.22.2-0 --namespace artifact-hub
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
After the upgrade, verify that the Artifact Hub pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If the Artifact Hub pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors that may indicate the cause of the failure.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Artifact Hub service, ensure that the service is running and check for any network policies that might be blocking access.
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace artifact-hub
kubectl describe svc artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient resources
troubleshooting.description:
If the pods are not starting due to insufficient resources, check the resource requests and limits set in the deployment and adjust them accordingly.
```bash
kubectl describe deployment artifact-hub --namespace artifact-hub
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release not found
troubleshooting.description:
If you encounter an error stating that the Helm release is not found, verify that you are using the correct namespace and release name.
```bash
helm list --namespace artifact-hub
```

prerequisites.description: You need a Kubernetes cluster and the Helm and kubectl command-line tools installed on your local machine.
